paper_structure.problem_statement: First-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions using adaptive estimates of lower-order moments (first moment / mean and second raw moment / uncentered variance of the gradient), with per-parameter adaptive learning rates and bias-corrected moment estimates.

reproduction_targets.target: Adam update rule (Algorithm 1) correctly minimizes a convex stochastic objective and matches the analytic/reference optimum
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: On L2-regularized multiclass logistic regression (convex), Adam converges to a training loss within tolerance of a high-accuracy reference optimizer (L-BFGS) optimum; parameter gradient norm at the found solution is small.

evidence.quote: m_t <- b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t; v_t <- b2*v_{t-1} + (1-b2)*g_t^2; mhat=m_t/(1-b1^t); vhat=v_t/(1-b2^t); theta_t <- theta_{t-1} - alpha*mhat/(sqrt(vhat)+eps)

reproduction_targets.target: Figure 1 (left): MNIST multiclass logistic regression training-cost convergence ranking
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Over equal epochs with alpha_t = alpha/sqrt(t) decay and minibatch 128, Adam yields similar final training cost to SGD+Nesterov (within a tolerance) and both reach lower training cost faster than AdaGrad.

evidence.quote: we found that the Adam yields similar convergence as SGD with momentum and both converge faster than Adagrad.

reproduction_targets.success_criteria: AdaMax (infinity-norm variant) also minimizes the convex objective; its u_t recursion equals max(beta2*u_{t-1}, |g_t|) and it requires no second-moment bias correction.

reproduction_targets.target: Initialization bias correction is necessary (Section 3 / Figure 4 qualitative claim)
reproduction_targets.success_criteria: Removing bias correction with beta2 close to 1 produces visibly larger/unstable early steps vs the bias-corrected version; bias-corrected v_t expectation tracks E[g^2].

evidence.quote: values beta2 close to 1 indeed lead to instabilities in training when no bias correction term was present, especially at first few epochs
Require: alpha: Stepsize. Good default settings ... alpha=0.001

contracts.ambiguity_audit:
# Ambiguity Audit: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization (Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015)

Paper type: algorithm (optimization algorithm) with an ml_experiment evaluation
Official code: NONE located/needed — Algorithm 1 (Adam) and Algorithm 2 (AdaMax) are fully specified in the paper text, including default hyperparameters.

## Core items

| Item | Status | Quote / Source | Our Choice | Alternatives |
|------|--------|----------------|------------|--------------|
| Adam update equations | SPECIFIED | Algorithm 1: `m_t=b1 m_{t-1}+(1-b1)g_t`, `v_t=b2 v_{t-1}+(1-b2)g_t^2`, `mhat=m/(1-b1^t)`, `vhat=v/(1-b2^t)`, `theta-=alpha*mhat/(sqrt(vhat)+eps)` | Implement exactly as written | efficient reorder (Sec 2 alt form) |
| Default hyperparameters | SPECIFIED | "alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8" (Algorithm 1 caption) | Use as defaults | — |
| Moment init | SPECIFIED | "m_0<-0, v_0<-0" | Zero init + bias correction | — |
| eps placement | SPECIFIED | `sqrt(vhat)+eps` (eps OUTSIDE the sqrt) | eps outside sqrt | eps inside sqrt (later TF variant) — NOT used |
| AdaMax update | SPECIFIED | Algorithm 2: `u_t=max(b2 u_{t-1},|g_t|)`, `theta-=(alpha/(1-b1^t)) m_t/u_t`; defaults alpha=0.002, b1=0.9, b2=0.999 | Implement exactly; no v-bias correction | — |
| Evaluation criterion | SPECIFIED (qualitative) | Fig 1: "training negative log likelihood"; Sec 6.1 ranking claim | Lower training cost; relative ranking Adam~SGDmom>AdaGrad | — |
| Random seeds | UNSPECIFIED | Paper does not fix seeds | Fix seed=0 for reproducibility | — |
| Hardware constraints | SPECIFIED (none relevant) | First-order, low memory, CPU-friendly | Run on CPU | — |

## Domain-specific items (ml_experiment)

| Item | Status | Quote / Source | Our Choice | Alternatives |
|------|--------|----------------|------------|--------------|
| Model | SPECIFIED | Sec 6.1 "L2-regularized multi-class logistic regression ... classifies the class label directly on the 784 dimension image vectors" | Multinomial logistic regression, 784->10 (or d->10) | — |
| Dataset | SPECIFIED | "using the MNIST dataset" | MNIST via download; fallback load_digits | — |
| Stepsize schedule | SPECIFIED | "alpha_t = alpha/sqrt(t) ... matches our theoretical findings" | alpha/sqrt(t) decay for ALL optimizers in this experiment | constant alpha |
| Minibatch size | SPECIFIED | "minibatch size of 128" | 128 | — |
| Baselines | SPECIFIED | "accelerated SGD with Nesterov momentum and Adagrad" | SGD+Nesterov, AdaGrad | — |
| L2 regularization lambda | UNSPECIFIED | "L2-regularized" but value not given | lambda=1e-4 (mild) | 0, 1e-3 |
| Nesterov momentum mu | UNSPECIFIED | "Nesterov momentum" value not given | mu=0.9 (standard) | 0.95, 0.99 |
| Per-optimizer learning rates | PARTIALLY_SPECIFIED | "hyper-parameters ... searched over a dense grid ... best ... setting" — best values not reported | Small per-optimizer grid search over base alpha, pick best-by-final-cost | use Adam defaults for all (unfair) |
| Reported metric numbers | UNSPECIFIED (none given) | Fig 1 has curves only, no table of numbers | Success = relative ranking + Adam reaches low-cost threshold no slower than SGD-mom | exact loss match (impossible) |
| Activation / nonlinearity | N/A | Logistic regression has none (softmax output) | softmax | — |

## algorithm-specific items

| Item | Status | Quote / Source | Our Choice | Alternatives |
|------|--------|----------------|------------|--------------|
| Termination | SPECIFIED | "while theta_t not converged do" | Fixed epoch budget (matches experiment) | gradient-norm threshold |
| Convergence/regret claim | SPECIFIED | Thm 4.1 / Cor 4.2: regret O(sqrt T), avg regret -> 0 | Optional: demonstrate average-regret decay on online convex loss | — |
| Effective step bound | SPECIFIED | Sec 2.1: `|Delta_t| <~ alpha` (trust region) | Used as sanity check | — |

## Contradictions found
- None material. The efficient reordered update in Section 2 (`alpha_t = alpha*sqrt(1-b2^t)/(1-b1^t)`) is mathematically equivalent to Algorithm 1; we implement the clear Algorithm 1 form and note equivalence.

## UNSPECIFIED items that will become ASSUMPTION comments in code
- L2 lambda: 1e-4 (alternatives: 0, 1e-3)
- Nesterov momentum mu: 0.9 (alternatives: 0.95, 0.99)
- Random seed: 0
- Per-optimizer base learning rate: chosen by a small grid search per optimizer (paper searched a dense grid but did not report the winners)
- Success criterion is a RELATIVE ranking because Figure 1 reports no numeric values

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate standard project skeleton from structure config JSON."""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "output"


def _dump_json(path: Path, payload: dict) -> None:
    path.write_text(
        json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )


def _as_list(config: dict, key: str) -> list:
    value = config.get(key, [])
    if value is None:
        return []
    return value if isinstance(value, list) else [value]


def build_reproduction_contract(config: dict) -> dict:
    """Create the stable contract every reproduction project is evaluated against."""
    return {
        "paper_title": config.get("paper_title") or config.get("source_pdf") or "unknown",
        "algorithm_name": config.get("algorithm_name") or config.get("project_name"),
        "reproduction_level": config.get("reproduction_level", "Level 3"),
        "reproduction_targets": _as_list(config, "reproduction_targets"),
        "datasets": _as_list(config, "datasets"),
        "metrics": _as_list(config, "metrics"),
        "baselines": _as_list(config, "baselines"),
        "hyperparameters": _as_list(config, "hyperparameters"),
        "experiments": _as_list(config, "experiments"),
        "assumptions": _as_list(config, "assumptions"),
        "missing_but_required": _as_list(config, "missing_but_required"),
        "status_schema": {
            "fully_reproduced": [],
            "approximately_reproduced": [],
            "not_reproduced": [],
        },
    }


def render_gap_report(contract: dict) -> str:
    gaps = contract.get("missing_but_required", [])
    assumptions = contract.get("assumptions", [])
    lines = [
        "# Gap Report",
        "",
        f"Paper: {contract.get('paper_title', 'unknown')}",
        f"Algorithm/System: {contract.get('algorithm_name', 'unknown')}",
        f"Target level: {contract.get('reproduction_level', 'Level 3')}",
        "",
        "## Blocking Or Risky Gaps",
    ]
    if gaps:
        for gap in gaps:
            if isinstance(gap, dict):
                severity = gap.get("severity", "unknown")
                item = gap.get("item", gap.get("name", "unknown"))
                impact = gap.get("impact", "impact not specified")
                fallback = gap.get("fallback", "fallback not specified")
                lines.append(f"- [{severity}] {item}: {impact} (fallback: {fallback})")
            else:
                lines.append(f"- {gap}")
    else:
        lines.append("- None recorded yet.")

    lines.extend(["", "## Assumptions"])
    if assumptions:
        for assumption in assumptions:
            lines.append(f"- {assumption}")
    else:
        lines.append("- None recorded yet.")
    lines.append("")
    return "\n".join(lines)

    targets = contract.get("reproduction_targets", [])

    lines = [

def scaffold(config: dict, output_dir: Path = None) -> Path:
    if output_dir is None:
        output_dir = DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR
    output_dir = Path(output_dir)

    project_name = config["project_name"].replace(" ", "_").lower()
    language = config.get("language", "python").lower()
    modules = config.get("modules", [])
    dependencies = config.get("dependencies", [])
    datasets = config.get("datasets", [])
    metrics = config.get("metrics", [])
    experiments = config.get("experiments", [])

"""Adam (Algorithm 1) and AdaMax (Algorithm 2) from Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015.

Implemented directly from the paper pseudocode. All operations are element-wise
over a flat parameter vector. These optimizers are parameter-shape agnostic: pass
in the current gradient via ``step(grad)`` and receive the parameter delta.
"""
import numpy as np


class Adam:
    """Adam optimizer, Algorithm 1.

    Update rule (paper, Section 2 / Algorithm 1):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        v_t = b2*v_{t-1} + (1-b2)*g_t^2
        mhat = m_t / (1 - b1^t)
        vhat = v_t / (1 - b2^t)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - alpha * mhat / (sqrt(vhat) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8):
        # SPECIFIED: defaults alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8 [Algorithm 1 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.eps = eps
        # SPECIFIED: m_0 <- 0, v_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 1)
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        """Return the parameter delta (to be added to theta) for one gradient.

        ``alpha`` may be overridden per step to implement the alpha/sqrt(t)
        schedule used in the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).
        """
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        mhat = self.m / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        vhat = self.v / (1.0 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
        # NOTE: eps is OUTSIDE the sqrt, exactly as written in Algorithm 1
        # (sqrt(vhat) + eps), not the later sqrt(vhat + eps) variant.
        return -a * mhat / (np.sqrt(vhat) + self.eps)


class AdaMax:
    """AdaMax optimizer, Algorithm 2 (infinity-norm variant of Adam).

    Update rule (paper, Section 7.1 / Algorithm 2):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        u_t = max(b2*u_{t-1}, |g_t|)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - (alpha / (1 - b1^t)) * m_t / u_t
    No second-moment bias correction is needed (u_t is an infinity norm).
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999):
        # SPECIFIED: AdaMax defaults alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999 [Algorithm 2 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.u = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)  # u_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 2)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        # u_t = max(beta2 * u_{t-1}, |g_t|)  -- element-wise (Algorithm 2)
        self.u = np.maximum(self.beta2 * self.u, np.abs(g))
        lr = a / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        # Guard against u_t == 0 at the very first step before any nonzero grad.
        denom = np.where(self.u > 0.0, self.u, 1.0)
        return -lr * self.m / denom

"""Adam (Algorithm 1) and AdaMax (Algorithm 2) from Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015.

Implemented directly from the paper pseudocode. All operations are element-wise
over a flat parameter vector. These optimizers are parameter-shape agnostic: pass
in the current gradient via ``step(grad)`` and receive the parameter delta.
"""
import numpy as np


class Adam:
    """Adam optimizer, Algorithm 1.

    Update rule (paper, Section 2 / Algorithm 1):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        v_t = b2*v_{t-1} + (1-b2)*g_t^2
        mhat = m_t / (1 - b1^t)
        vhat = v_t / (1 - b2^t)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - alpha * mhat / (sqrt(vhat) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8):
        # SPECIFIED: defaults alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8 [Algorithm 1 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.eps = eps
        # SPECIFIED: m_0 <- 0, v_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 1)
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        """Return the parameter delta (added to theta) for one gradient.

        ``alpha`` may be overridden per step to implement the alpha/sqrt(t)
        schedule used in the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).
        """
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        mhat = self.m / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        vhat = self.v / (1.0 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
        # NOTE: eps is OUTSIDE the sqrt, exactly as written in Algorithm 1:
        # (sqrt(vhat) + eps), not the later sqrt(vhat + eps) variant.
        return -a * mhat / (np.sqrt(vhat) + self.eps)


class AdaMax:
    """AdaMax optimizer, Algorithm 2 (infinity-norm variant of Adam).

    Update rule (paper, Section 7.1 / Algorithm 2):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        u_t = max(b2*u_{t-1}, |g_t|)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - (alpha / (1 - b1^t)) * m_t / u_t
    No second-moment bias correction is needed (u_t is an infinity norm).
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999):
        # SPECIFIED: AdaMax defaults alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999 [Algorithm 2 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.u = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)  # u_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 2)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        # u_t = max(beta2 * u_{t-1}, |g_t|)  -- element-wise (Algorithm 2)
        self.u = np.maximum(self.beta2 * self.u, np.abs(g))
        lr = a / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        # Guard against u_t == 0 before any nonzero gradient is seen.
        denom = np.where(self.u > 0.0, self.u, 1.0)
        return -lr * self.m / denom


class AdamNoBiasCorrection(Adam):
    """Adam without initialization bias correction (Section 3 / 6.4 ablation).

    Identical to Adam but skips the /(1-b^t) terms. Used to demonstrate that,
    with beta2 close to 1, the missing correction produces much larger early
    steps (paper: "lack of initialisation bias correction would lead to initial
    steps that are much larger").
    """

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        # No bias correction:
        return -a * self.m / (np.sqrt(self.v) + self.eps)

"""Baseline optimizers used in Section 6.1: SGD+Nesterov momentum and AdaGrad.

Implemented in numpy with the same ``step(grad, alpha=None)`` interface as the
Adam module so the experiment loop can treat all optimizers uniformly.
"""
import numpy as np


class SGDNesterov:
    """SGD with Nesterov momentum.

    ASSUMPTION: the paper says "accelerated SGD with Nesterov momentum" but does
    not give the momentum coefficient; we use mu=0.9 (standard). [see ambiguity_audit.md]

    Nesterov update (velocity form):
        v_t   = mu*v_{t-1} - lr*g_t
        delta = mu*v_t - lr*g_t      (look-ahead applied to the step)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.01, mu=0.9):
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.mu = mu
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = self.mu * self.v - a * g
        return self.mu * self.v - a * g


class AdaGrad:
    """AdaGrad (Duchi et al., 2011).

    Accumulates squared gradients; per-parameter stepsize ~ 1/sqrt(sum g^2).
        G_t   = G_{t-1} + g_t^2
        delta = -lr * g_t / (sqrt(G_t) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.01, eps=1e-8):
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.eps = eps
        self.G = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.G += g * g
        return -a * g / (np.sqrt(self.G) + self.eps)

"""Metrics and reproduction-criterion helpers.

Figure 1 (left) reports only qualitative training-cost curves (no numbers), so
the success criteria are expressed as relative comparisons between optimizers,
plus an absolute correctness check against a high-accuracy reference optimum.
"""
import numpy as np


def epochs_to_threshold(cost_curve, threshold):
    """First epoch index (1-based) at which cost_curve <= threshold, else None."""
    for i, c in enumerate(cost_curve):
        if c <= threshold:
            return i + 1
    return None


def final_cost(cost_curve, tail=3):
    """Mean of the last ``tail`` epoch costs (robust final value)."""
    arr = np.asarray(cost_curve, dtype=np.float64)
    return float(arr[-tail:].mean())


def relative_gap(a, b):
    """|a - b| / max(|b|, eps)."""
    return float(abs(a - b) / max(abs(b), 1e-12))


def check_figure1_ranking(final_costs, threshold, curves):
    """Evaluate the Figure-1 (left) convergence-ranking criterion.

    final_costs: {optimizer_name: final_cost}
    curves:      {optimizer_name: cost_curve}
    Criterion (from Section 6.1, paper text):
      1. Adam final cost ~ SGD+Nesterov final cost (within tolerance).
      2. Both Adam and SGD+Nesterov reach `threshold` no later than AdaGrad
         (faster convergence than AdaGrad).
    """
    adam_f = final_costs["Adam"]
    sgd_f = final_costs["SGDNesterov"]
    ada_f = final_costs["AdaGrad"]

    # 1. similar convergence Adam ~ SGD-momentum
    similar = relative_gap(adam_f, sgd_f) <= 0.15  # 15% tolerance on final cost

    # 2. faster than AdaGrad
    e_adam = epochs_to_threshold(curves["Adam"], threshold)
    e_sgd = epochs_to_threshold(curves["SGDNesterov"], threshold)
    e_ada = epochs_to_threshold(curves["AdaGrad"], threshold)
    big = 10 ** 9
    e_adam_v = e_adam if e_adam is not None else big
    e_sgd_v = e_sgd if e_sgd is not None else big
    e_ada_v = e_ada if e_ada is not None else big
    faster_than_adagrad = (e_adam_v <= e_ada_v) and (e_sgd_v <= e_ada_v)
    # Also require Adam/SGD to actually end below AdaGrad's final cost.
    lower_final = (adam_f <= ada_f) and (sgd_f <= ada_f)

    return {
        "adam_similar_to_sgd_momentum": bool(similar),
        "adam_sgd_faster_than_adagrad": bool(faster_than_adagrad and lower_final),
        "final_costs": {k: round(v, 6) for k, v in final_costs.items()},
        "epochs_to_threshold": {
            "threshold": threshold,
            "Adam": e_adam, "SGDNesterov": e_sgd, "AdaGrad": e_ada,
        },
        "relative_gap_adam_vs_sgd": round(relative_gap(adam_f, sgd_f), 4),
    }

"""Dataset loaders for the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).

Tries to load real MNIST (784-dim, the paper's dataset). If no network / cache
is available, falls back to scikit-learn's load_digits (64-dim, 10 classes) --
the same convex multiclass logistic-regression setting in which the optimizer
convergence-ranking claim is preserved. The substitution is recorded in the
returned metadata and in gap_report.md.
"""
import numpy as np


def _standardize(Xtr, Xte):
    mu = Xtr.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    sd = Xtr.std(axis=0, keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    return (Xtr - mu) / sd, (Xte - mu) / sd


def load_synthetic(n=2048, d=50, k=10, seed=0):
    """Separable-ish synthetic multiclass data for smoke/correctness tests."""
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    Wtrue = rng.normal(size=(d, k))
    X = rng.normal(size=(n, d))
    logits = X @ Wtrue
    y = logits.argmax(axis=1)
    split = int(0.8 * n)
    return (X[:split], y[:split], X[split:], y[split:],
            {"name": "synthetic", "source": "generated", "n_features": d, "n_classes": k})


def _try_mnist():
    """Return (Xtr,ytr,Xte,yte) for real MNIST or raise on failure."""
    try:
        from sklearn.datasets import fetch_openml
        mn = fetch_openml("mnist_784", version=1, as_frame=False, parser="auto")
        X = mn.data.astype(np.float64) / 255.0
        y = mn.target.astype(int)
        Xtr, ytr = X[:60000], y[:60000]
        Xte, yte = X[60000:], y[60000:]
        return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte
    except Exception as e:  # network / cache unavailable
        raise RuntimeError(f"MNIST unavailable: {e}")


def load_classification(prefer="mnist", subsample=None, seed=0):
    """Load the experiment dataset with graceful fallback.

    Returns (Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta). ``meta['name']`` is one of
    'mnist', 'digits', 'synthetic'; ``meta['is_substitute']`` flags fallbacks.
    """
    meta = {}
    if prefer == "synthetic":
        Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, m = load_synthetic(seed=seed)
        m["is_substitute"] = False
        return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, m

    if prefer == "mnist":
        try:
            Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte = _try_mnist()
            meta = {"name": "mnist", "source": "fetch_openml('mnist_784')",
                    "n_features": 784, "n_classes": 10, "is_substitute": False}
        except Exception:
            # Fallback: load_digits (8x8). Same convex setting, smaller.
            from sklearn.datasets import load_digits
            d = load_digits()
            X = d.data.astype(np.float64)
            y = d.target.astype(int)
            rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
            idx = rng.permutation(len(X))
            X, y = X[idx], y[idx]
            split = int(0.8 * len(X))
            Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte = X[:split], y[:split], X[split:], y[split:]
            Xtr, Xte = _standardize(Xtr, Xte)
            meta = {"name": "digits", "source": "sklearn.load_digits (MNIST substitute)",
                    "n_features": Xtr.shape[1], "n_classes": 10, "is_substitute": True,
                    "substitute_reason": "MNIST download unavailable; load_digits is the same "
                                         "convex multiclass logistic-regression setting."}
    if meta.get("name") == "mnist":
        Xtr, Xte = _standardize(Xtr, Xte)

    if subsample is not None and subsample < len(Xtr):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        sel = rng.choice(len(Xtr), size=subsample, replace=False)
        Xtr, ytr = Xtr[sel], ytr[sel]
        meta["subsampled_train"] = int(subsample)

    return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta

"""Shared minibatch training loop for the Section 6.1 experiment."""
import numpy as np


def train_optimizer(model, optimizer, Xtr, ytr, epochs, batch_size=128,
                    lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", base_alpha=None, seed=0):
    """Train ``model`` with ``optimizer`` and record per-epoch training cost.

    lr_schedule:
      'inv_sqrt_t' -> alpha_t = base_alpha / sqrt(t)  (Section 6.1, t = global step)
      'constant'   -> alpha_t = base_alpha
    Returns (theta, cost_curve list of length `epochs`).
    """
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    theta = model.init_params(seed=seed)
    n = Xtr.shape[0]
    cost_curve = []
    t = 0  # global step counter for the schedule
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        perm = rng.permutation(n)
        for start in range(0, n, batch_size):
            idx = perm[start:start + batch_size]
            _, grad = model.loss_and_grad(theta, Xtr[idx], ytr[idx])
            t += 1
            if base_alpha is None:
                alpha = None
            elif lr_schedule == "inv_sqrt_t":
                # SPECIFIED: alpha_t = alpha / sqrt(t) (Section 6.1)
                alpha = base_alpha / np.sqrt(t)
            else:
                alpha = base_alpha
            theta = theta + optimizer.step(grad, alpha=alpha)
        # full-train-set cost at end of epoch (matches Figure 1 "training cost")
        cost_curve.append(model.loss(theta, Xtr, ytr))
    return theta, cost_curve

"""Fast import / data / algorithm-correctness smoke test.

Checks (Level 2 algorithm correctness):
  1. All modules import.
  2. Adam minimizes a convex logistic-regression objective to within tolerance
     of a high-accuracy reference optimum (scipy L-BFGS), proving the update
     rule is correct -- not merely that "code runs".
  3. AdaMax also converges, and its u_t recursion equals max(b2*u, |g|).
  4. Bias-corrected E[v_t] tracks (1 - b2^t) * E[g^2] (Section 3, eq. 4).
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

from src.adam import Adam, AdaMax  # noqa: E402
from src.logistic_regression import LogisticRegression  # noqa: E402
from src.data.loaders import load_synthetic  # noqa: E402
from src.experiments.train import train_optimizer  # noqa: E402


def reference_optimum(model, X, y):
    """High-accuracy convex optimum via scipy L-BFGS for ground truth."""
    from scipy.optimize import minimize
    theta0 = model.init_params()
    res = minimize(lambda th: model.loss_and_grad(th, X, y)[0], theta0,
                   jac=lambda th: model.loss_and_grad(th, X, y)[1],
                   method="L-BFGS-B", options={"maxiter": 500})
    return float(res.fun)


def main():
    out = {"checks": {}}
    Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta = load_synthetic(n=2048, d=50, k=10, seed=0)
    model = LogisticRegression(n_features=50, n_classes=10, l2=1e-4)

    ref = reference_optimum(model, Xtr, ytr)
    out["reference_optimum_loss"] = round(ref, 6)

    # 2. Adam -> reference optimum
    adam = Adam(model.dim, alpha=0.05)
    _, adam_curve = train_optimizer(model, adam, Xtr, ytr, epochs=60,
                                    batch_size=128, base_alpha=0.05,
                                    lr_schedule="constant", seed=0)
    adam_final = float(adam_curve[-1])
    adam_gap = abs(adam_final - ref) / max(abs(ref), 1e-9)
    out["checks"]["adam_reaches_reference_optimum"] = bool(adam_gap < 0.05)
    out["adam_final_loss"] = round(adam_final, 6)
    out["adam_rel_gap_to_reference"] = round(adam_gap, 5)
    out["adam_loss_monotone_trend"] = bool(adam_curve[-1] < adam_curve[0])

    # 3. AdaMax converges + recursion identity
    amax = AdaMax(model.dim, alpha=0.05)
    _, amax_curve = train_optimizer(model, amax, Xtr, ytr, epochs=60,
                                    batch_size=128, base_alpha=0.05,
                                    lr_schedule="constant", seed=0)
    amax_gap = abs(float(amax_curve[-1]) - ref) / max(abs(ref), 1e-9)
    out["checks"]["adamax_reaches_reference_optimum"] = bool(amax_gap < 0.10)
    out["adamax_final_loss"] = round(float(amax_curve[-1]), 6)

    # explicit u_t = max(b2 u_{t-1}, |g_t|) identity check
    rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
    chk = AdaMax(3, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9)
    u_ref = np.zeros(3)
    ok_u = True
    for _ in range(20):
        g = rng.normal(size=3)
        chk.step(g)
        u_ref = np.maximum(0.9 * u_ref, np.abs(g))
        if not np.allclose(chk.u, u_ref):
            ok_u = False
    out["checks"]["adamax_u_recursion_correct"] = bool(ok_u)

    # 4. bias-correction expectation: E[v_t] ~ (1-b2^t) E[g^2] for constant-var g
    b2 = 0.999
    sigma2 = 4.0
    rng = np.random.default_rng(2)
    trials = 4000
    t_check = 50
    vs = []
    for _ in range(trials):
        v = 0.0
        for _ in range(t_check):
            g = rng.normal(0.0, np.sqrt(sigma2))
            v = b2 * v + (1 - b2) * g * g
        vs.append(v)
    emp = float(np.mean(vs))
    theory = (1 - b2 ** t_check) * sigma2
    out["bias_correction"] = {"empirical_E_v": round(emp, 5),
                              "theory_(1-b2^t)E[g2]": round(theory, 5),
                              "rel_err": round(abs(emp - theory) / theory, 3)}
    out["checks"]["bias_correction_expectation_tracks"] = bool(
        abs(emp - theory) / theory < 0.05)

    all_pass = all(out["checks"].values())
    out["status"] = "ok" if all_pass else "failed"
    (ROOT / "results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    (ROOT / "results" / "smoke_summary.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(out, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    for k, v in out["checks"].items():
        print(f"  [{'PASS' if v else 'FAIL'}] {k}")
    print(f"SMOKE {'OK' if all_pass else 'FAILED'}: "
          f"Adam final={out['adam_final_loss']} ref={out['reference_optimum_loss']} "
          f"(rel gap {out['adam_rel_gap_to_reference']})")
    if not all_pass:
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Reproduce Figure 1 (left): MNIST logistic-regression convergence ranking.

Compares Adam vs SGD+Nesterov vs AdaGrad on L2-regularized multiclass logistic
regression with minibatch 128 and the alpha_t = alpha/sqrt(t) schedule
(Section 6.1). Per-optimizer base learning rates are chosen by a small grid
search (paper searched a dense grid but did not report the winners).

Also runs two extension targets: AdaMax (Algorithm 2) and the bias-correction
ablation (Section 6.4).
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

from src.adam import Adam, AdaMax, AdamNoBiasCorrection  # noqa: E402
from src.baselines import SGDNesterov, AdaGrad  # noqa: E402
from src.logistic_regression import LogisticRegression  # noqa: E402
from src.data.loaders import load_classification  # noqa: E402
from src.experiments.train import train_optimizer  # noqa: E402
from src.metrics import final_cost  # noqa: E402

EPOCHS = 45            # paper uses 45 epochs for MNIST logistic regression (Fig 1 x-axis)
BATCH = 128            # SPECIFIED minibatch size (Section 6.1)
L2 = 1e-4             # ASSUMPTION (see ambiguity_audit.md)
SEED = 0

# Small per-optimizer LR grids (paper: dense grid search, winners unreported).
GRIDS = {
    "Adam": (Adam, [0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02]),
    "SGDNesterov": (SGDNesterov, [0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.3]),
    "AdaGrad": (AdaGrad, [0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1]),
}


def run_one(model, opt_cls, base_alpha, Xtr, ytr):
    opt = opt_cls(model.dim)
    _, curve = train_optimizer(model, opt, Xtr, ytr, epochs=EPOCHS,
                               batch_size=BATCH, base_alpha=base_alpha,
                               lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    return curve


def tune_and_run(name, opt_cls, grid, model, Xtr, ytr):
    best = None
    for a in grid:
        curve = run_one(model, opt_cls, a, Xtr, ytr)
        fc = final_cost(curve)
        if (not np.isfinite(fc)):
            continue
        if best is None or fc < best[1]:
            best = (a, fc, curve)
    return best  # (best_alpha, final_cost, curve)


def main():
    # Subsample train for speed if real MNIST is large; digits is already small.
    Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta = load_classification(prefer="mnist",
                                                   subsample=12000, seed=SEED)
    n_features = Xtr.shape[1]
    model = LogisticRegression(n_features=n_features, n_classes=10, l2=L2)

    curves, final_costs, best_alphas = {}, {}, {}
    for name, (cls, grid) in GRIDS.items():
        a, fc, curve = tune_and_run(name, cls, grid, model, Xtr, ytr)
        curves[name] = [float(c) for c in curve]
        final_costs[name] = float(fc)
        best_alphas[name] = a
        print(f"  {name:12s} best_alpha={a:<7} final_cost={fc:.5f}")

    # Extension: AdaMax (Algorithm 2)
    amax_a, amax_fc, amax_curve = tune_and_run(
        "AdaMax", AdaMax, [0.002, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02], model, Xtr, ytr)
    curves["AdaMax"] = [float(c) for c in amax_curve]
    final_costs["AdaMax"] = float(amax_fc)
    best_alphas["AdaMax"] = amax_a

    # Extension: bias-correction ablation (beta2 close to 1 -> larger early steps)
    abc = Adam(model.dim, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9999)
    _, with_bc = train_optimizer(model, abc, Xtr, ytr, epochs=10, batch_size=BATCH,
                                 base_alpha=0.01, lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    anbc = AdamNoBiasCorrection(model.dim, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9999)
    _, no_bc = train_optimizer(model, anbc, Xtr, ytr, epochs=10, batch_size=BATCH,
                               base_alpha=0.01, lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    # First-epoch cost: without correction, beta2~1 inflates the early step.
    bias_ablation = {
        "beta2": 0.9999,
        "with_correction_epoch1_cost": float(with_bc[0]),
        "without_correction_epoch1_cost": float(no_bc[0]),
        "without_correction_unstable_early": bool(no_bc[0] > with_bc[0]),
    }

    # Threshold for "epochs to reach low cost": 110% of the best final cost
    # achieved by any of the three main optimizers (a low-cost target).
    best_final = min(final_costs[k] for k in GRIDS)
    threshold = best_final * 1.10

    summary = {
        "status": "ok",
        "dataset": meta,
        "epochs": EPOCHS,
        "batch_size": BATCH,
        "l2": L2,
        "lr_schedule": "alpha/sqrt(t)",
        "best_alphas": best_alphas,
        "final_costs": final_costs,
        "curves": curves,
        "threshold_for_convergence_speed": threshold,
        "adamax": {"best_alpha": amax_a, "final_cost": amax_fc},
        "bias_correction_ablation": bias_ablation,
    }
    results_dir = ROOT / "results"
    results_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    (results_dir / "reproduction_summary.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(summary, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    _save_figure(curves, meta, threshold)
    print(f"WROTE {results_dir/'reproduction_summary.json'}")


def _save_figure(curves, meta, threshold):
    try:
        import matplotlib
        matplotlib.use("Agg")
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        fig_dir = ROOT / "figures"
        fig_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
        styles = {"AdaGrad": "C0", "SGDNesterov": "C2", "Adam": "C3",
                  "AdaMax": "C1"}
        for name, curve in curves.items():
            xs = range(1, len(curve) + 1)
            plt.plot(xs, curve, label=name, color=styles.get(name))
        plt.axhline(threshold, ls="--", lw=0.8, color="gray",
                    label="conv. threshold")
        plt.xlabel("epochs over entire dataset")
        plt.ylabel("training cost")
        ds = meta.get("name", "data")
        plt.title(f"{ds} Logistic Regression (repro of Fig 1 left)")
        plt.legend(fontsize=8)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(fig_dir / "figure1_left.png", dpi=120)
        plt.close()
        print(f"WROTE {fig_dir/'figure1_left.png'}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[warn] figure not saved: {e}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Evaluate experiment + smoke outputs against reproduction_contract.json.

Success criteria are FIXED by the contract (Phase 2). This script only reads
result artifacts and classifies each target as fully / approximately / not
reproduced. It never edits the contract.
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
from src.metrics import check_figure1_ranking  # noqa: E402


def main():
    contract = json.loads((ROOT / "reproduction_contract.json").read_text())
    res_path = ROOT / "results" / "reproduction_summary.json"
    smoke_path = ROOT / "results" / "smoke_summary.json"
    if not res_path.exists():
        raise SystemExit("Missing results/reproduction_summary.json. Run run_experiment.py.")
    summary = json.loads(res_path.read_text())
    smoke = json.loads(smoke_path.read_text()) if smoke_path.exists() else {}

    full, approx, notrepro = [], [], []

    # Target 1: Adam minimizes convex objective to reference optimum (smoke)
    if smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adam_reaches_reference_optimum"):
        full.append({"target": "Adam (Algorithm 1) reaches convex reference optimum",
                     "evidence": {"adam_final_loss": smoke.get("adam_final_loss"),
                                  "reference_optimum_loss": smoke.get("reference_optimum_loss"),
                                  "rel_gap": smoke.get("adam_rel_gap_to_reference")}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Adam reaches convex reference optimum",
                         "reason": "smoke check failed or missing"})

    # Target 2: Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking
    ranking = check_figure1_ranking(
        summary["final_costs"], summary["threshold_for_convergence_speed"],
        summary["curves"])
    cond1 = ranking["adam_similar_to_sgd_momentum"]
    cond2 = ranking["adam_sgd_faster_than_adagrad"]
    if cond1 and cond2:
        full.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking "
                               "(Adam ~ SGD+Nesterov > AdaGrad)", "evidence": ranking})
    elif cond1 or cond2:
        approx.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking",
                       "reason": "one of two sub-criteria met", "evidence": ranking})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking",
                         "reason": "neither sub-criterion met", "evidence": ranking})

    # Target 3: AdaMax correctness
    amax_ok = smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adamax_reaches_reference_optimum") and \
        smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adamax_u_recursion_correct")
    if amax_ok:
        full.append({"target": "AdaMax (Algorithm 2) correctness",
                     "evidence": {"u_recursion": smoke["checks"]["adamax_u_recursion_correct"],
                                  "adamax_final_loss": smoke.get("adamax_final_loss"),
                                  "experiment_final_cost": summary.get("adamax", {}).get("final_cost")}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "AdaMax correctness", "reason": "smoke checks failed"})

    # Target 4: bias-correction necessity
    bc_smoke = smoke.get("checks", {}).get("bias_correction_expectation_tracks")
    ablation = summary.get("bias_correction_ablation", {})
    bc_ablation = ablation.get("without_correction_unstable_early")
    if bc_smoke and bc_ablation:
        full.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity (Sec 3 / 6.4)",
                     "evidence": {"expectation_tracks": bc_smoke,
                                  "ablation": ablation}})
    elif bc_smoke or bc_ablation:
        approx.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity",
                       "evidence": {"expectation_tracks": bc_smoke, "ablation": ablation}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity",
                         "reason": "neither expectation-tracking nor ablation showed the effect"})

    evaluation = {
        "target_level": contract.get("reproduction_level"),
        "dataset_used": summary.get("dataset", {}),
        "fully_reproduced": full,
        "approximately_reproduced": approx,
        "not_reproduced": notrepro,
    }
    out = ROOT / "results" / "reproduction_evaluation.json"
    out.write_text(json.dumps(evaluation, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    print("=== Reproduction Evaluation ===")
    print(f"  fully_reproduced:        {len(full)}")
    print(f"  approximately_reproduced:{len(approx)}")
    print(f"  not_reproduced:          {len(notrepro)}")
    for t in full:
        print(f"   [FULL ] {t['target']}")
    for t in approx:
        print(f"   [APPROX] {t['target']}")
    for t in notrepro:
        print(f"   [NOT  ] {t['target']}")
    print(f"WROTE {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Official code: NONE located/needed — Algorithm 1 (Adam) and Algorithm 2 (AdaMax) are fully specified in the paper text, including default hyperparameters.

"""Adam (Algorithm 1) and AdaMax (Algorithm 2) from Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015.

Implemented directly from the paper pseudocode. All operations are element-wise
over a flat parameter vector. These optimizers are parameter-shape agnostic: pass
in the current gradient via ``step(grad)`` and receive the parameter delta.
"""
import numpy as np


class Adam:
    """Adam optimizer, Algorithm 1.

    Update rule (paper, Section 2 / Algorithm 1):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        v_t = b2*v_{t-1} + (1-b2)*g_t^2
        mhat = m_t / (1 - b1^t)
        vhat = v_t / (1 - b2^t)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - alpha * mhat / (sqrt(vhat) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8):
        # SPECIFIED: defaults alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8 [Algorithm 1 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.eps = eps
        # SPECIFIED: m_0 <- 0, v_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 1)
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        """Return the parameter delta (to be added to theta) for one gradient.

        ``alpha`` may be overridden per step to implement the alpha/sqrt(t)
        schedule used in the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).
        """
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        mhat = self.m / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        vhat = self.v / (1.0 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
        # NOTE: eps is OUTSIDE the sqrt, exactly as written in Algorithm 1
        # (sqrt(vhat) + eps), not the later sqrt(vhat + eps) variant.
        return -a * mhat / (np.sqrt(vhat) + self.eps)


class AdaMax:
    """AdaMax optimizer, Algorithm 2 (infinity-norm variant of Adam).

    Update rule (paper, Section 7.1 / Algorithm 2):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        u_t = max(b2*u_{t-1}, |g_t|)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - (alpha / (1 - b1^t)) * m_t / u_t
    No second-moment bias correction is needed (u_t is an infinity norm).
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999):
        # SPECIFIED: AdaMax defaults alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999 [Algorithm 2 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.u = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)  # u_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 2)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        # u_t = max(beta2 * u_{t-1}, |g_t|)  -- element-wise (Algorithm 2)
        self.u = np.maximum(self.beta2 * self.u, np.abs(g))
        lr = a / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        # Guard against u_t == 0 at the very first step before any nonzero grad.
        denom = np.where(self.u > 0.0, self.u, 1.0)
        return -lr * self.m / denom

"""Adam (Algorithm 1) and AdaMax (Algorithm 2) from Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015.

Implemented directly from the paper pseudocode. All operations are element-wise
over a flat parameter vector. These optimizers are parameter-shape agnostic: pass
in the current gradient via ``step(grad)`` and receive the parameter delta.
"""
import numpy as np


class Adam:
    """Adam optimizer, Algorithm 1.

    Update rule (paper, Section 2 / Algorithm 1):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        v_t = b2*v_{t-1} + (1-b2)*g_t^2
        mhat = m_t / (1 - b1^t)
        vhat = v_t / (1 - b2^t)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - alpha * mhat / (sqrt(vhat) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8):
        # SPECIFIED: defaults alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8 [Algorithm 1 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.eps = eps
        # SPECIFIED: m_0 <- 0, v_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 1)
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        """Return the parameter delta (added to theta) for one gradient.

        ``alpha`` may be overridden per step to implement the alpha/sqrt(t)
        schedule used in the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).
        """
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        mhat = self.m / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        vhat = self.v / (1.0 - self.beta2 ** self.t)
        # NOTE: eps is OUTSIDE the sqrt, exactly as written in Algorithm 1:
        # (sqrt(vhat) + eps), not the later sqrt(vhat + eps) variant.
        return -a * mhat / (np.sqrt(vhat) + self.eps)


class AdaMax:
    """AdaMax optimizer, Algorithm 2 (infinity-norm variant of Adam).

    Update rule (paper, Section 7.1 / Algorithm 2):
        m_t = b1*m_{t-1} + (1-b1)*g_t
        u_t = max(b2*u_{t-1}, |g_t|)
        theta_t = theta_{t-1} - (alpha / (1 - b1^t)) * m_t / u_t
    No second-moment bias correction is needed (u_t is an infinity norm).
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999):
        # SPECIFIED: AdaMax defaults alpha=0.002, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999 [Algorithm 2 caption]
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.beta1 = beta1
        self.beta2 = beta2
        self.m = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)
        self.u = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)  # u_0 <- 0 (Algorithm 2)
        self.t = 0

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        # u_t = max(beta2 * u_{t-1}, |g_t|)  -- element-wise (Algorithm 2)
        self.u = np.maximum(self.beta2 * self.u, np.abs(g))
        lr = a / (1.0 - self.beta1 ** self.t)
        # Guard against u_t == 0 before any nonzero gradient is seen.
        denom = np.where(self.u > 0.0, self.u, 1.0)
        return -lr * self.m / denom


class AdamNoBiasCorrection(Adam):
    """Adam without initialization bias correction (Section 3 / 6.4 ablation).

    Identical to Adam but skips the /(1-b^t) terms. Used to demonstrate that,
    with beta2 close to 1, the missing correction produces much larger early
    steps (paper: "lack of initialisation bias correction would lead to initial
    steps that are much larger").
    """

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        self.t += 1
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.m = self.beta1 * self.m + (1.0 - self.beta1) * g
        self.v = self.beta2 * self.v + (1.0 - self.beta2) * (g * g)
        # No bias correction:
        return -a * self.m / (np.sqrt(self.v) + self.eps)

"""Baseline optimizers used in Section 6.1: SGD+Nesterov momentum and AdaGrad.

Implemented in numpy with the same ``step(grad, alpha=None)`` interface as the
Adam module so the experiment loop can treat all optimizers uniformly.
"""
import numpy as np


class SGDNesterov:
    """SGD with Nesterov momentum.

    ASSUMPTION: the paper says "accelerated SGD with Nesterov momentum" but does
    not give the momentum coefficient; we use mu=0.9 (standard). [see ambiguity_audit.md]

    Nesterov update (velocity form):
        v_t   = mu*v_{t-1} - lr*g_t
        delta = mu*v_t - lr*g_t      (look-ahead applied to the step)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.01, mu=0.9):
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.mu = mu
        self.v = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.v = self.mu * self.v - a * g
        return self.mu * self.v - a * g


class AdaGrad:
    """AdaGrad (Duchi et al., 2011).

    Accumulates squared gradients; per-parameter stepsize ~ 1/sqrt(sum g^2).
        G_t   = G_{t-1} + g_t^2
        delta = -lr * g_t / (sqrt(G_t) + eps)
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, alpha=0.01, eps=1e-8):
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.eps = eps
        self.G = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float64)

    def step(self, grad, alpha=None):
        a = self.alpha if alpha is None else alpha
        g = np.asarray(grad, dtype=np.float64)
        self.G += g * g
        return -a * g / (np.sqrt(self.G) + self.eps)

"""Metrics and reproduction-criterion helpers.

Figure 1 (left) reports only qualitative training-cost curves (no numbers), so
the success criteria are expressed as relative comparisons between optimizers,
plus an absolute correctness check against a high-accuracy reference optimum.
"""
import numpy as np


def epochs_to_threshold(cost_curve, threshold):
    """First epoch index (1-based) at which cost_curve <= threshold, else None."""
    for i, c in enumerate(cost_curve):
        if c <= threshold:
            return i + 1
    return None


def final_cost(cost_curve, tail=3):
    """Mean of the last ``tail`` epoch costs (robust final value)."""
    arr = np.asarray(cost_curve, dtype=np.float64)
    return float(arr[-tail:].mean())


def relative_gap(a, b):
    """|a - b| / max(|b|, eps)."""
    return float(abs(a - b) / max(abs(b), 1e-12))


def check_figure1_ranking(final_costs, threshold, curves):
    """Evaluate the Figure-1 (left) convergence-ranking criterion.

    final_costs: {optimizer_name: final_cost}
    curves:      {optimizer_name: cost_curve}
    Criterion (from Section 6.1, paper text):
      1. Adam final cost ~ SGD+Nesterov final cost (within tolerance).
      2. Both Adam and SGD+Nesterov reach `threshold` no later than AdaGrad
         (faster convergence than AdaGrad).
    """
    adam_f = final_costs["Adam"]
    sgd_f = final_costs["SGDNesterov"]
    ada_f = final_costs["AdaGrad"]

    # 1. similar convergence Adam ~ SGD-momentum
    similar = relative_gap(adam_f, sgd_f) <= 0.15  # 15% tolerance on final cost

    # 2. faster than AdaGrad
    e_adam = epochs_to_threshold(curves["Adam"], threshold)
    e_sgd = epochs_to_threshold(curves["SGDNesterov"], threshold)
    e_ada = epochs_to_threshold(curves["AdaGrad"], threshold)
    big = 10 ** 9
    e_adam_v = e_adam if e_adam is not None else big
    e_sgd_v = e_sgd if e_sgd is not None else big
    e_ada_v = e_ada if e_ada is not None else big
    faster_than_adagrad = (e_adam_v <= e_ada_v) and (e_sgd_v <= e_ada_v)
    # Also require Adam/SGD to actually end below AdaGrad's final cost.
    lower_final = (adam_f <= ada_f) and (sgd_f <= ada_f)

    return {
        "adam_similar_to_sgd_momentum": bool(similar),
        "adam_sgd_faster_than_adagrad": bool(faster_than_adagrad and lower_final),
        "final_costs": {k: round(v, 6) for k, v in final_costs.items()},
        "epochs_to_threshold": {
            "threshold": threshold,
            "Adam": e_adam, "SGDNesterov": e_sgd, "AdaGrad": e_ada,
        },
        "relative_gap_adam_vs_sgd": round(relative_gap(adam_f, sgd_f), 4),
    }

"""Dataset loaders for the logistic-regression experiment (Section 6.1).

Tries to load real MNIST (784-dim, the paper's dataset). If no network / cache
is available, falls back to scikit-learn's load_digits (64-dim, 10 classes) --
the same convex multiclass logistic-regression setting in which the optimizer
convergence-ranking claim is preserved. The substitution is recorded in the
returned metadata and in gap_report.md.
"""
import numpy as np


def _standardize(Xtr, Xte):
    mu = Xtr.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    sd = Xtr.std(axis=0, keepdims=True) + 1e-8
    return (Xtr - mu) / sd, (Xte - mu) / sd


def load_synthetic(n=2048, d=50, k=10, seed=0):
    """Separable-ish synthetic multiclass data for smoke/correctness tests."""
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    Wtrue = rng.normal(size=(d, k))
    X = rng.normal(size=(n, d))
    logits = X @ Wtrue
    y = logits.argmax(axis=1)
    split = int(0.8 * n)
    return (X[:split], y[:split], X[split:], y[split:],
            {"name": "synthetic", "source": "generated", "n_features": d, "n_classes": k})


def _try_mnist():
    """Return (Xtr,ytr,Xte,yte) for real MNIST or raise on failure."""
    try:
        from sklearn.datasets import fetch_openml
        mn = fetch_openml("mnist_784", version=1, as_frame=False, parser="auto")
        X = mn.data.astype(np.float64) / 255.0
        y = mn.target.astype(int)
        Xtr, ytr = X[:60000], y[:60000]
        Xte, yte = X[60000:], y[60000:]
        return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte
    except Exception as e:  # network / cache unavailable
        raise RuntimeError(f"MNIST unavailable: {e}")


def load_classification(prefer="mnist", subsample=None, seed=0):
    """Load the experiment dataset with graceful fallback.

    Returns (Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta). ``meta['name']`` is one of
    'mnist', 'digits', 'synthetic'; ``meta['is_substitute']`` flags fallbacks.
    """
    meta = {}
    if prefer == "synthetic":
        Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, m = load_synthetic(seed=seed)
        m["is_substitute"] = False
        return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, m

    if prefer == "mnist":
        try:
            Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte = _try_mnist()
            meta = {"name": "mnist", "source": "fetch_openml('mnist_784')",
                    "n_features": 784, "n_classes": 10, "is_substitute": False}
        except Exception:
            # Fallback: load_digits (8x8). Same convex setting, smaller.
            from sklearn.datasets import load_digits
            d = load_digits()
            X = d.data.astype(np.float64)
            y = d.target.astype(int)
            rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
            idx = rng.permutation(len(X))
            X, y = X[idx], y[idx]
            split = int(0.8 * len(X))
            Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte = X[:split], y[:split], X[split:], y[split:]
            Xtr, Xte = _standardize(Xtr, Xte)
            meta = {"name": "digits", "source": "sklearn.load_digits (MNIST substitute)",
                    "n_features": Xtr.shape[1], "n_classes": 10, "is_substitute": True,
                    "substitute_reason": "MNIST download unavailable; load_digits is the same "
                                         "convex multiclass logistic-regression setting."}
    if meta.get("name") == "mnist":
        Xtr, Xte = _standardize(Xtr, Xte)

    if subsample is not None and subsample < len(Xtr):
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        sel = rng.choice(len(Xtr), size=subsample, replace=False)
        Xtr, ytr = Xtr[sel], ytr[sel]
        meta["subsampled_train"] = int(subsample)

    return Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta

"""Shared minibatch training loop for the Section 6.1 experiment."""
import numpy as np


def train_optimizer(model, optimizer, Xtr, ytr, epochs, batch_size=128,
                    lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", base_alpha=None, seed=0):
    """Train ``model`` with ``optimizer`` and record per-epoch training cost.

    lr_schedule:
      'inv_sqrt_t' -> alpha_t = base_alpha / sqrt(t)  (Section 6.1, t = global step)
      'constant'   -> alpha_t = base_alpha
    Returns (theta, cost_curve list of length `epochs`).
    """
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
    theta = model.init_params(seed=seed)
    n = Xtr.shape[0]
    cost_curve = []
    t = 0  # global step counter for the schedule
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        perm = rng.permutation(n)
        for start in range(0, n, batch_size):
            idx = perm[start:start + batch_size]
            _, grad = model.loss_and_grad(theta, Xtr[idx], ytr[idx])
            t += 1
            if base_alpha is None:
                alpha = None
            elif lr_schedule == "inv_sqrt_t":
                # SPECIFIED: alpha_t = alpha / sqrt(t) (Section 6.1)
                alpha = base_alpha / np.sqrt(t)
            else:
                alpha = base_alpha
            theta = theta + optimizer.step(grad, alpha=alpha)
        # full-train-set cost at end of epoch (matches Figure 1 "training cost")
        cost_curve.append(model.loss(theta, Xtr, ytr))
    return theta, cost_curve

"""Fast import / data / algorithm-correctness smoke test.

Checks (Level 2 algorithm correctness):
  1. All modules import.
  2. Adam minimizes a convex logistic-regression objective to within tolerance
     of a high-accuracy reference optimum (scipy L-BFGS), proving the update
     rule is correct -- not merely that "code runs".
  3. AdaMax also converges, and its u_t recursion equals max(b2*u, |g|).
  4. Bias-corrected E[v_t] tracks (1 - b2^t) * E[g^2] (Section 3, eq. 4).
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

from src.adam import Adam, AdaMax  # noqa: E402
from src.logistic_regression import LogisticRegression  # noqa: E402
from src.data.loaders import load_synthetic  # noqa: E402
from src.experiments.train import train_optimizer  # noqa: E402


def reference_optimum(model, X, y):
    """High-accuracy convex optimum via scipy L-BFGS for ground truth."""
    from scipy.optimize import minimize
    theta0 = model.init_params()
    res = minimize(lambda th: model.loss_and_grad(th, X, y)[0], theta0,
                   jac=lambda th: model.loss_and_grad(th, X, y)[1],
                   method="L-BFGS-B", options={"maxiter": 500})
    return float(res.fun)


def main():
    out = {"checks": {}}
    Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta = load_synthetic(n=2048, d=50, k=10, seed=0)
    model = LogisticRegression(n_features=50, n_classes=10, l2=1e-4)

    ref = reference_optimum(model, Xtr, ytr)
    out["reference_optimum_loss"] = round(ref, 6)

    # 2. Adam -> reference optimum
    adam = Adam(model.dim, alpha=0.05)
    _, adam_curve = train_optimizer(model, adam, Xtr, ytr, epochs=60,
                                    batch_size=128, base_alpha=0.05,
                                    lr_schedule="constant", seed=0)
    adam_final = float(adam_curve[-1])
    adam_gap = abs(adam_final - ref) / max(abs(ref), 1e-9)
    out["checks"]["adam_reaches_reference_optimum"] = bool(adam_gap < 0.05)
    out["adam_final_loss"] = round(adam_final, 6)
    out["adam_rel_gap_to_reference"] = round(adam_gap, 5)
    out["adam_loss_monotone_trend"] = bool(adam_curve[-1] < adam_curve[0])

    # 3. AdaMax converges + recursion identity
    amax = AdaMax(model.dim, alpha=0.05)
    _, amax_curve = train_optimizer(model, amax, Xtr, ytr, epochs=60,
                                    batch_size=128, base_alpha=0.05,
                                    lr_schedule="constant", seed=0)
    amax_gap = abs(float(amax_curve[-1]) - ref) / max(abs(ref), 1e-9)
    out["checks"]["adamax_reaches_reference_optimum"] = bool(amax_gap < 0.10)
    out["adamax_final_loss"] = round(float(amax_curve[-1]), 6)

    # explicit u_t = max(b2 u_{t-1}, |g_t|) identity check
    rng = np.random.default_rng(1)
    chk = AdaMax(3, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9)
    u_ref = np.zeros(3)
    ok_u = True
    for _ in range(20):
        g = rng.normal(size=3)
        chk.step(g)
        u_ref = np.maximum(0.9 * u_ref, np.abs(g))
        if not np.allclose(chk.u, u_ref):
            ok_u = False
    out["checks"]["adamax_u_recursion_correct"] = bool(ok_u)

    # 4. bias-correction expectation: E[v_t] ~ (1-b2^t) E[g^2] for constant-var g
    b2 = 0.999
    sigma2 = 4.0
    rng = np.random.default_rng(2)
    trials = 4000
    t_check = 50
    vs = []
    for _ in range(trials):
        v = 0.0
        for _ in range(t_check):
            g = rng.normal(0.0, np.sqrt(sigma2))
            v = b2 * v + (1 - b2) * g * g
        vs.append(v)
    emp = float(np.mean(vs))
    theory = (1 - b2 ** t_check) * sigma2
    out["bias_correction"] = {"empirical_E_v": round(emp, 5),
                              "theory_(1-b2^t)E[g2]": round(theory, 5),
                              "rel_err": round(abs(emp - theory) / theory, 3)}
    out["checks"]["bias_correction_expectation_tracks"] = bool(
        abs(emp - theory) / theory < 0.05)

    all_pass = all(out["checks"].values())
    out["status"] = "ok" if all_pass else "failed"
    (ROOT / "results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    (ROOT / "results" / "smoke_summary.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(out, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    for k, v in out["checks"].items():
        print(f"  [{'PASS' if v else 'FAIL'}] {k}")
    print(f"SMOKE {'OK' if all_pass else 'FAILED'}: "
          f"Adam final={out['adam_final_loss']} ref={out['reference_optimum_loss']} "
          f"(rel gap {out['adam_rel_gap_to_reference']})")
    if not all_pass:
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Reproduce Figure 1 (left): MNIST logistic-regression convergence ranking.

Compares Adam vs SGD+Nesterov vs AdaGrad on L2-regularized multiclass logistic
regression with minibatch 128 and the alpha_t = alpha/sqrt(t) schedule
(Section 6.1). Per-optimizer base learning rates are chosen by a small grid
search (paper searched a dense grid but did not report the winners).

Also runs two extension targets: AdaMax (Algorithm 2) and the bias-correction
ablation (Section 6.4).
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

from src.adam import Adam, AdaMax, AdamNoBiasCorrection  # noqa: E402
from src.baselines import SGDNesterov, AdaGrad  # noqa: E402
from src.logistic_regression import LogisticRegression  # noqa: E402
from src.data.loaders import load_classification  # noqa: E402
from src.experiments.train import train_optimizer  # noqa: E402
from src.metrics import final_cost  # noqa: E402

EPOCHS = 45            # paper uses 45 epochs for MNIST logistic regression (Fig 1 x-axis)
BATCH = 128            # SPECIFIED minibatch size (Section 6.1)
L2 = 1e-4             # ASSUMPTION (see ambiguity_audit.md)
SEED = 0

# Small per-optimizer LR grids (paper: dense grid search, winners unreported).
GRIDS = {
    "Adam": (Adam, [0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02]),
    "SGDNesterov": (SGDNesterov, [0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.3]),
    "AdaGrad": (AdaGrad, [0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1]),
}


def run_one(model, opt_cls, base_alpha, Xtr, ytr):
    opt = opt_cls(model.dim)
    _, curve = train_optimizer(model, opt, Xtr, ytr, epochs=EPOCHS,
                               batch_size=BATCH, base_alpha=base_alpha,
                               lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    return curve


def tune_and_run(name, opt_cls, grid, model, Xtr, ytr):
    best = None
    for a in grid:
        curve = run_one(model, opt_cls, a, Xtr, ytr)
        fc = final_cost(curve)
        if (not np.isfinite(fc)):
            continue
        if best is None or fc < best[1]:
            best = (a, fc, curve)
    return best  # (best_alpha, final_cost, curve)


def main():
    # Subsample train for speed if real MNIST is large; digits is already small.
    Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte, meta = load_classification(prefer="mnist",
                                                   subsample=12000, seed=SEED)
    n_features = Xtr.shape[1]
    model = LogisticRegression(n_features=n_features, n_classes=10, l2=L2)

    curves, final_costs, best_alphas = {}, {}, {}
    for name, (cls, grid) in GRIDS.items():
        a, fc, curve = tune_and_run(name, cls, grid, model, Xtr, ytr)
        curves[name] = [float(c) for c in curve]
        final_costs[name] = float(fc)
        best_alphas[name] = a
        print(f"  {name:12s} best_alpha={a:<7} final_cost={fc:.5f}")

    # Extension: AdaMax (Algorithm 2)
    amax_a, amax_fc, amax_curve = tune_and_run(
        "AdaMax", AdaMax, [0.002, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02], model, Xtr, ytr)
    curves["AdaMax"] = [float(c) for c in amax_curve]
    final_costs["AdaMax"] = float(amax_fc)
    best_alphas["AdaMax"] = amax_a

    # Extension: bias-correction ablation (beta2 close to 1 -> larger early steps)
    abc = Adam(model.dim, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9999)
    _, with_bc = train_optimizer(model, abc, Xtr, ytr, epochs=10, batch_size=BATCH,
                                 base_alpha=0.01, lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    anbc = AdamNoBiasCorrection(model.dim, alpha=0.01, beta2=0.9999)
    _, no_bc = train_optimizer(model, anbc, Xtr, ytr, epochs=10, batch_size=BATCH,
                               base_alpha=0.01, lr_schedule="inv_sqrt_t", seed=SEED)
    # First-epoch cost: without correction, beta2~1 inflates the early step.
    bias_ablation = {
        "beta2": 0.9999,
        "with_correction_epoch1_cost": float(with_bc[0]),
        "without_correction_epoch1_cost": float(no_bc[0]),
        "without_correction_unstable_early": bool(no_bc[0] > with_bc[0]),
    }

    # Threshold for "epochs to reach low cost": 110% of the best final cost
    # achieved by any of the three main optimizers (a low-cost target).
    best_final = min(final_costs[k] for k in GRIDS)
    threshold = best_final * 1.10

    summary = {
        "status": "ok",
        "dataset": meta,
        "epochs": EPOCHS,
        "batch_size": BATCH,
        "l2": L2,
        "lr_schedule": "alpha/sqrt(t)",
        "best_alphas": best_alphas,
        "final_costs": final_costs,
        "curves": curves,
        "threshold_for_convergence_speed": threshold,
        "adamax": {"best_alpha": amax_a, "final_cost": amax_fc},
        "bias_correction_ablation": bias_ablation,
    }
    results_dir = ROOT / "results"
    results_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    (results_dir / "reproduction_summary.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(summary, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    _save_figure(curves, meta, threshold)
    print(f"WROTE {results_dir/'reproduction_summary.json'}")


def _save_figure(curves, meta, threshold):
    try:
        import matplotlib
        matplotlib.use("Agg")
        import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
        fig_dir = ROOT / "figures"
        fig_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
        styles = {"AdaGrad": "C0", "SGDNesterov": "C2", "Adam": "C3",
                  "AdaMax": "C1"}
        for name, curve in curves.items():
            xs = range(1, len(curve) + 1)
            plt.plot(xs, curve, label=name, color=styles.get(name))
        plt.axhline(threshold, ls="--", lw=0.8, color="gray",
                    label="conv. threshold")
        plt.xlabel("epochs over entire dataset")
        plt.ylabel("training cost")
        ds = meta.get("name", "data")
        plt.title(f"{ds} Logistic Regression (repro of Fig 1 left)")
        plt.legend(fontsize=8)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(fig_dir / "figure1_left.png", dpi=120)
        plt.close()
        print(f"WROTE {fig_dir/'figure1_left.png'}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"[warn] figure not saved: {e}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Evaluate experiment + smoke outputs against reproduction_contract.json.

Success criteria are FIXED by the contract (Phase 2). This script only reads
result artifacts and classifies each target as fully / approximately / not
reproduced. It never edits the contract.
"""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
from src.metrics import check_figure1_ranking  # noqa: E402


def main():
    contract = json.loads((ROOT / "reproduction_contract.json").read_text())
    res_path = ROOT / "results" / "reproduction_summary.json"
    smoke_path = ROOT / "results" / "smoke_summary.json"
    if not res_path.exists():
        raise SystemExit("Missing results/reproduction_summary.json. Run run_experiment.py.")
    summary = json.loads(res_path.read_text())
    smoke = json.loads(smoke_path.read_text()) if smoke_path.exists() else {}

    full, approx, notrepro = [], [], []

    # Target 1: Adam minimizes convex objective to reference optimum (smoke)
    if smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adam_reaches_reference_optimum"):
        full.append({"target": "Adam (Algorithm 1) reaches convex reference optimum",
                     "evidence": {"adam_final_loss": smoke.get("adam_final_loss"),
                                  "reference_optimum_loss": smoke.get("reference_optimum_loss"),
                                  "rel_gap": smoke.get("adam_rel_gap_to_reference")}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Adam reaches convex reference optimum",
                         "reason": "smoke check failed or missing"})

    # Target 2: Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking
    ranking = check_figure1_ranking(
        summary["final_costs"], summary["threshold_for_convergence_speed"],
        summary["curves"])
    cond1 = ranking["adam_similar_to_sgd_momentum"]
    cond2 = ranking["adam_sgd_faster_than_adagrad"]
    if cond1 and cond2:
        full.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking "
                               "(Adam ~ SGD+Nesterov > AdaGrad)", "evidence": ranking})
    elif cond1 or cond2:
        approx.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking",
                       "reason": "one of two sub-criteria met", "evidence": ranking})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Figure 1 (left) convergence ranking",
                         "reason": "neither sub-criterion met", "evidence": ranking})

    # Target 3: AdaMax correctness
    amax_ok = smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adamax_reaches_reference_optimum") and \
        smoke.get("checks", {}).get("adamax_u_recursion_correct")
    if amax_ok:
        full.append({"target": "AdaMax (Algorithm 2) correctness",
                     "evidence": {"u_recursion": smoke["checks"]["adamax_u_recursion_correct"],
                                  "adamax_final_loss": smoke.get("adamax_final_loss"),
                                  "experiment_final_cost": summary.get("adamax", {}).get("final_cost")}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "AdaMax correctness", "reason": "smoke checks failed"})

    # Target 4: bias-correction necessity
    bc_smoke = smoke.get("checks", {}).get("bias_correction_expectation_tracks")
    ablation = summary.get("bias_correction_ablation", {})
    bc_ablation = ablation.get("without_correction_unstable_early")
    if bc_smoke and bc_ablation:
        full.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity (Sec 3 / 6.4)",
                     "evidence": {"expectation_tracks": bc_smoke,
                                  "ablation": ablation}})
    elif bc_smoke or bc_ablation:
        approx.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity",
                       "evidence": {"expectation_tracks": bc_smoke, "ablation": ablation}})
    else:
        notrepro.append({"target": "Initialization bias correction necessity",
                         "reason": "neither expectation-tracking nor ablation showed the effect"})

    evaluation = {
        "target_level": contract.get("reproduction_level"),
        "dataset_used": summary.get("dataset", {}),
        "fully_reproduced": full,
        "approximately_reproduced": approx,
        "not_reproduced": notrepro,
    }
    out = ROOT / "results" / "reproduction_evaluation.json"
    out.write_text(json.dumps(evaluation, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    print("=== Reproduction Evaluation ===")
    print(f"  fully_reproduced:        {len(full)}")
    print(f"  approximately_reproduced:{len(approx)}")
    print(f"  not_reproduced:          {len(notrepro)}")
    for t in full:
        print(f"   [FULL ] {t['target']}")
    for t in approx:
        print(f"   [APPROX] {t['target']}")
    for t in notrepro:
        print(f"   [NOT  ] {t['target']}")
    print(f"WROTE {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate standard project skeleton from structure config JSON."""
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path

DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "output"


def _dump_json(path: Path, payload: dict) -> None:
    path.write_text(
        json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n",
        encoding="utf-8",
    )


def _as_list(config: dict, key: str) -> list:
    value = config.get(key, [])
    if value is None:
        return []
    return value if isinstance(value, list) else [value]


def build_reproduction_contract(config: dict) -> dict:
    """Create the stable contract every reproduction project is evaluated against."""
    return {
        "paper_title": config.get("paper_title") or config.get("source_pdf") or "unknown",
        "algorithm_name": config.get("algorithm_name") or config.get("project_name"),
        "reproduction_level": config.get("reproduction_level", "Level 3"),
        "reproduction_targets": _as_list(config, "reproduction_targets"),
        "datasets": _as_list(config, "datasets"),
        "metrics": _as_list(config, "metrics"),
        "baselines": _as_list(config, "baselines"),
        "hyperparameters": _as_list(config, "hyperparameters"),
        "experiments": _as_list(config, "experiments"),
        "assumptions": _as_list(config, "assumptions"),
        "missing_but_required": _as_list(config, "missing_but_required"),
        "status_schema": {
            "fully_reproduced": [],
            "approximately_reproduced": [],
            "not_reproduced": [],
        },
    }


def render_gap_report(contract: dict) -> str:
    gaps = contract.get("missing_but_required", [])
    assumptions = contract.get("assumptions", [])
    lines = [
        "# Gap Report",
        "",
        f"Paper: {contract.get('paper_title', 'unknown')}",
        f"Algorithm/System: {contract.get('algorithm_name', 'unknown')}",
        f"Target level: {contract.get('reproduction_level', 'Level 3')}",
        "",
        "## Blocking Or Risky Gaps",
    ]
    if gaps:
        for gap in gaps:
            if isinstance(gap, dict):
                severity = gap.get("severity", "unknown")
                item = gap.get("item", gap.get("name", "unknown"))
                impact = gap.get("impact", "impact not specified")
                fallback = gap.get("fallback", "fallback not specified")
                lines.append(f"- [{severity}] {item}: {impact} (fallback: {fallback})")
            else:
                lines.append(f"- {gap}")
    else:
        lines.append("- None recorded yet.")

    lines.extend(["", "## Assumptions"])
    if assumptions:
        for assumption in assumptions:
            lines.append(f"- {assumption}")
    else:
        lines.append("- None recorded yet.")
    lines.append("")
    return "\n".join(lines)

def scaffold(config: dict, output_dir: Path = None) -> Path:
    if output_dir is None:
        output_dir = DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR
    output_dir = Path(output_dir)

    project_name = config["project_name"].replace(" ", "_").lower()
    language = config.get("language", "python").lower()
    modules = config.get("modules", [])
    dependencies = config.get("dependencies", [])
    datasets = config.get("datasets", [])
    metrics = config.get("metrics", [])
    experiments = config.get("experiments", [])
    reproduction_level = config.get("reproduction_level", "Level 3")
    contract = build_reproduction_contract(config)
    paper_structure = config.get("paper_structure") or {
        "status": "placeholder",
        "note": "Replace with extracted paper_structure.json before implementation.",
    }

    project_dir = output_dir / project_name
    project_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    if language == "python":
        src_dir = project_dir / "src"
        src_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        (src_dir / "__init__.py").touch()

        for module in modules:
            name = module["name"].replace(" ", "_").lower()
            desc = module.get("description", name)

                f'"""{desc}"""\n', encoding="utf-8"

        for package in ["data", "evaluation", "experiments"]:
            package_dir = src_dir / package
            package_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
            (package_dir / "__init__.py").touch()

        configs_dir = project_dir / "configs"
        scripts_dir = project_dir / "scripts"
        results_dir = project_dir / "results"
        figures_dir = project_dir / "figures"
        for d in [configs_dir, scripts_dir, results_dir, figures_dir]:
            d.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

        _dump_json(
            configs_dir / "smoke.json",

                "num_samples": 16,

            },

        _dump_json(
            configs_dir / "reproduction.json",
            {
                "reproduction_level": reproduction_level,
                "datasets": datasets,
                "metrics": metrics,
                "experiments": experiments,
                "seed": 0,
            },
        )
        _dump_json(project_dir / "paper_structure.json", paper_structure)
        _dump_json(project_dir / "reproduction_contract.json", contract)
        (project_dir / "gap_report.md").write_text(

        (project_dir / "REPRODUCTION_REPORT.md").write_text(

        (scripts_dir / "run_smoke.py").write_text(
            '''"""End-to-end smoke test for the reproduction project."""\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport json\n\n\ndef main():\n    config_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "configs" / "smoke.json"\n    config = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))\n    print(f"SMOKE OK: synthetic samples={config.get('num_samples')} seed={config.get('seed')}")\n\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()\n''',
            encoding="utf-8",

reproduction_report.text:
# Adam (Adaptive Moment Estimation)

Implementation of: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization (Kingma & Ba, ICLR 2015)

## Reproduction Status

- Target level: Level 3
- Fully reproduced:
  - Adam (Algorithm 1) reaches a convex logistic-regression reference optimum.
  - Figure 1 (left) qualitative convergence ranking is reproduced on a bounded substitute run: Adam and SGD+Nesterov converge to similar final cost and both beat AdaGrad.
  - Initialization bias correction necessity is reproduced through the Section 3 expectation check and a beta2=0.9999 ablation.
- Approximately reproduced: none recorded by the evaluator.
- Not reproduced:
  - AdaMax correctness: the u_t recursion identity passed, but the smoke convergence-to-reference criterion failed.

## Reproduction Targets
- Adam update rule (Algorithm 1) minimizes a convex objective to the reference optimum
- Figure 1 (left) MNIST logistic regression convergence ranking
- AdaMax (Algorithm 2) correctness
- Initialization bias correction necessity (Sec 3 / 6.4)

## Experiments
- `scripts/run_smoke.py`: Adam / AdaMax update-rule checks, convex optimum comparison, and bias-correction expectation check.
- `scripts/run_experiment.py`: bounded logistic-regression convergence comparison for Adam, SGD+Nesterov, AdaGrad, and AdaMax.
- `scripts/evaluate_reproduction.py`: contract-based classification of the four reproduction targets.

## Results

- Smoke status: failed because `adamax_reaches_reference_optimum` failed.
- Adam final loss: 0.127799 versus reference optimum 0.123238, relative gap 0.03701.
- Experiment final costs:
  - Adam: 0.163607
  - SGD+Nesterov: 0.155643
  - AdaGrad: 0.289079
  - AdaMax: 0.227681
- Evaluator status:
  - Fully reproduced: 3 targets.
  - Approximately reproduced: 0 targets.
  - Not reproduced: 1 target.

## Gaps

The original paper's Figure 1 uses MNIST and a dense learning-rate grid, but the exact best learning rates and some implementation details are not reported. This run used a bounded grid and local dataset fallback strategy. The qualitative convergence-ranking target is therefore useful as a portfolio probe but should not be presented as an exact numerical reproduction of the original figure.

The AdaMax target exposed a concrete failure: the recurrence identity passed, but the convergence criterion did not. This is useful failure-label evidence rather than a reason to mark the run as successful.

## Usage

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python scripts/run_smoke.py
python scripts/run_experiment.py
python scripts/evaluate_reproduction.py
```

## Notes

Update this report after running the evaluator. Do not mark a target as reproduced until it is supported by an artifact and a contract criterion.

failure_analysis.evaluation_remedy: Keep exact-claim and approximate-claim checks separate so honest partial reproductions score above fake success but below exact reproduction.
